title: Modular Exponentiation
description: Efficiently compute large powers modulo n
mermaid: graph TD A1[Start: Input base (b), exponent (e), modulus (n)] style A1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff B1[Initialize: result = 1] style B1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 C1[e > 0 ?] style C1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 A1 --> B1 B1 --> C1 C1 -- Yes --> D1 D1[e is even?] style D1 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000 C1 -- No --> I1 D1 -- Yes --> E1 E1[b = (b * b) mod n] style E1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff F1[e = e / 2] style F1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff E1 --> F1 F1 --> C1 D1 -- No --> G1 G1[result = (result * b) mod n] style G1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff H1[e = e - 1] style H1 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff G1 --> H1 H1 --> C1 I1[Output: result] style I1 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff C1 -- No --> I1
source_text: Source information to be added - please specify the book, paper, or reference material used by the LLM to generate this flowchart.
